nameMeanings.name: Fitz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names В© Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z вҖҳsonвҖҷ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz вҖҳsonвҖҷ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Fitzgerald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z вҖҳsonвҖҷ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), вҖҳson of GeraldвҖҷ. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) вҖҳle GrosвҖҷ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299вҖ“2011.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z вҖҳsonвҖҷ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning вҖҳson of WalterвҖҷ.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Fitzherbert
meanings: English and Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z вҖҳsonвҖҷ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Herbert (see Herbert ), вҖҳson of HerbertвҖҷ.

variants.name: Fitzhenry
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z вҖҳsonвҖҷ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Henry (see Henry ), вҖҳson of HenryвҖҷ. This surname is common in Ireland (mainly Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as Mac ГүinrГӯ. In at least some cases, it seems to have been adopted as a surname indicating the illegitimate offspring of a man called Henry.

variants.name: Fitzsimmons
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Fitzsimon, with post-medieval excrescent -s, from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z вҖҳsonвҖҷ (see Fitz ) + either of the personal names Simon or Sigmund (see Simon ), вҖҳson of Simon or SigmundвҖҷ. The name is also found in the Gaelicized form Mac SГӯomГіin.

variants.name: Fitzgibbon
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z вҖҳsonвҖҷ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gibbon, a Norman French pet form of Gilbert ; вҖҳson of GibbonвҖҷ. This name has long been a widespread and important surname in Ireland, and Gaelicized as Mac GiobГәin. Sir Gilbert (Gibbon) FitzJohn, illegitimate son of John FitzGerald, 1st Baron Desmond (died 1261), was the ancestor of a line of Anglo-Norman aristocracy in Ireland, who acquired the surname Fitzgibbon with reference to him. Gilbert and his Fitzgibbon descendants were known as вҖҳthe White KnightsвҖҷ on account of their fair complexion.

variants.name: Fitzharris
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z вҖҳsonвҖҷ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Harry вҖҳson of HarryвҖҷ. MacLysaght describes this as the name of a distinguished County Wexford family, also known as Fitzhenry .

variants.name: Fitzmaurice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Ciaran, Colm.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z вҖҳsonвҖҷ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Maurice (see Morris 1), вҖҳson of MauriceвҖҷ. This surname is common in Ireland, where it has also been Gaelicized as Mac Muiris. The Irish family of Fitzmaurice share a common Norman ancestry with the Fitzgerald family. They can be traced to Thomas Fitzmaurice, whose brother Gerald was the ancestor of the Irish Fitzgerald dynasty. Their father was Maurice Fitzgerald, a Norman baron (died 1176).

variants.name: Fitzhugh
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z вҖҳsonвҖҷ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Hugh (see Hugh ), вҖҳson of HughвҖҷ.

History: William Fitzhugh (1651вҖ“1701), from Bedford, England, emigrated to VA c. 1670 and established himself on the Potomac River in what was then Stafford County, VA, as a planter and exporter. He also practiced law, was a member of the Virginia House of Burgesses, and served in 1687 as lieutenant colonel of the county militia.

variants.name: Fitzroy
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z вҖҳsonвҖҷ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz вҖҳsonвҖҷ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Martin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names В© Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, Irish, French, Walloon, Breton, Dutch, Flemish, German, Czech, Slovak, Croatian, Italian (Veneto); Spanish (MartГӯn): from a personal name derived from Latin Martinus, itself a derivative of Mars, genitive Martis, the Roman god of fertility and war, whose name may derive ultimately from a root mar вҖҳgleamвҖҷ. This was borne by a famous 4th-century Christian saint, Martin of Tours, and consequently became extremely popular throughout Europe in the Middle Ages. In North America, the surname Martin has absorbed cognates and derivatives from other languages, e.g. Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Marcin , Albanian Martini , Polish surnames beginning with Marcin-, and Slovenian patronymics like MartinДҚiДҚ (see Martincic ). Martin is the most frequent surname in France and one of the most frequent surnames in Wallonia.
English: variant of   Marton  .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Г“ MГЎrtain, вҖҳdescendant of MartinвҖҷ (compare 1 above). Otherwise, a shortened form of Gilmartin or McMartin ; sometimes also spelled Martyn.

variants.name: Marczewski
meanings: Polish: habitational name for someone from Marcewo (formerly also Marczewo) in Greater Poland Voivodeship. Alternatively, a derivative of the personal name Marc, Marcz, a pet form of Marcin, Marek (see Martin , Mark ).

variants.name: Marciniak
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Eugeniusz, Ewa, Agnieszka, Bartosz, Boleslaw, Ignatius, Jaroslaw, Jerzy, Krystyna, Ryszard, Slawomir, Stanislawa.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Marak
meanings: Czech:
(MarГЎk): from a pet form of the personal names Martin or Marek .
(MaЕҷГЎk): metronymic from the female personal name MГЎЕҷГӯ, Czech form of Maria . It may also denoted the husband of a woman named MГЎЕҷГӯ.

variants.name: Machain
meanings: Basque: Castilianized form of Matxain, a topographic or habitational name referring to a house named with a diminutive of the personal name Martin .

variants.name: Maertens
meanings: Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from the personal name Maerten, a variant of Martin .
North German (MГӨrtens): see Martens .

variants.name: Marcin
meanings: Slovak, Rusyn (from Slovakia), and Polish: from Marcin, a personal name equivalent to Martin . In Poland this is the most usual form as a personal name, but the surname is very rare there, while in Slovakia, where Marcin is a dialect form of the name Martin, the situation is opposite.
American shortened form of Polish Marcinkiewicz and other derivatives of the personal name Marcin (see 1 above).
In some cases possibly also French: from a vernacular form of the personal name Marcien, derived from the Latin surname Marcianus, popularized through the cult of a local saint.

variants.name: Macho
meanings: Spanish: nickname from macho вҖҳmale; muleвҖҷ (from Latin masculus вҖҳmale, masculineвҖҷ), applied either to denote a virile, strong man or alternatively an idiot.
Spanish: possibly also a nickname for a forceful person or a metonymic occupational name for a smith, from macho вҖҳsledgehammerвҖҷ (from Latin marculus вҖҳhammerвҖҷ).
Slovak: from a derivative of the personal name   Martin  .

variants.name: Marcinowski
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Aleksander, Bogdan, Jerzy, Zbigniew, Zigmund.
Polish: habitational name for someone from any of the places called MarcinГіw, Marcinowa, Marcinowice, or any of numerous other places in Poland named with the personal name Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Marcinkiewicz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Henryk, Beata, Elzbieta, Janina.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name Marcinek, a petform of Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Mar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Gim, Wai, Fong, Hong, Man Chung, Moo, Mun, Quon, Wing, Yat, Bok, Chun.
Spanish, Portuguese, Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Catalan: topographic name for someone living by the sea, from mar вҖҳseaвҖҷ (from Latin mare).
German: nickname from Middle High German mar(w) вҖҳtender, delicateвҖҷ, or an altered form of Mahr, a topographic name from mar вҖҳlake, swamp, stagnant waterвҖҷ.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names В© Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil вҖҳwill, desireвҖҷ + helm вҖҳhelmet, protectionвҖҷ. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the вҖҳconqueredвҖҷ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Fitz Martin was born about 1170, in Dartington, Devon, England, United Kingdom, his father, William Fitz Martin Lord of Cemais, was 37 and his mother, Angharad Ankaret ferch Rhys, was 32. He had at least 1 son with Avice de BrГ©autГ©. He died on 15 February 1216, in Blagdon, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 47.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Fitz Martin est nГ© environ 1170, Г  Dartington, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son pГЁre, William Fitz Martin Lord of Cemais, avait 37 ans et sa mГЁre, Angharad Ankaret ferch Rhys, avait 32 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Avice de BrГ©autГ©. Il est dГ©cГ©dГ© le 15 fГ©vrier 1216, Г  Blagdon, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, Г  lвҖҷГўge de 47 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Fitz Martin um 1170 in Dartington, Devon, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Fitz Martin Lord of Cemais 37 und seine Mutter Angharad Ankaret ferch Rhys 32. Er hatte mit Avice de BrГ©autГ© mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 15. Februar 1216 im Alter von 47 Jahren in Blagdon, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Fitz Martin ГЁ nato il circa 1170 nel Dartington, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, William Fitz Martin Lord of Cemais, aveva 37 anni e il suo madre, Angharad Ankaret ferch Rhys, aveva 32 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Avice de BrГ©autГ©. MorГ¬ il 15 febbraio 1216, nel Blagdon, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'etГ  di 47 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: William Fitz MartinгҒҢзҙ„1170е№ҙгҒ«гӮӨгӮ®гғӘгӮ№гӮӨгӮ®гғӘгӮ№гғҮгғңгғігғҖгғ«гғҶгӮЈгғігғҲгғігҒ§з”ҹгҒҫгӮҢгҒҹжҷӮпјҢзҲ¶William Fitz Martin Lord of CemaisгҒҜ37жӯіпјҢжҜҚAngharad Ankaret ferch RhysгҒҜ32жӯігҒ§гҒӮгҒЈгҒҹгҖӮеҪјгҒҜAvice de BrГ©autГ©гҒЁгҒ®й–“гҒ«е°‘гҒӘгҒҸгҒЁгӮӮ1дәәгҒ®жҒҜеӯҗгӮ’гӮӮгҒҶгҒ‘гҒҹгҖӮеҪјгҒҜ1216е№ҙ2жңҲ15ж—ҘгҒ«гӮӨгӮ®гғӘгӮ№гӮӨгӮ®гғӘгӮ№гӮҪгғЎгғ«гӮ»гғҲгғ–гғ©гӮ°гғүгғігҒ§47жӯігҒ§жӯ»еҺ»гҒ—гҒҹгҖӮ

ko.lifeSummary: William Fitz Martin(мқҙ)к°Җ лҢҖлһө 1170л…„ мҳҒкөӯ мһүкёҖлһңл“ң лҚ°нҸ° лҚ”лҘҙнӢҙк·ён„ҙм—җм„ң нғңм–ҙлӮ¬мқ„ л•Ң, к·ёмқҳ м•„лІ„м§Җ William Fitz Martin Lord of Cemais(мқҖ)лҠ” 37м„ёмҳҖкі , к·ёмқҳ м–ҙлЁёлӢҲ Angharad Ankaret ferch Rhys(мқҖ)лҠ” 32м„ёмҳҖлӢӨ. к·ёлҠ” Avice de BrГ©autГ©(мҷҖ)кіј м Ғм–ҙлҸ„ н•ң лӘ…мқҳ м•„л“Өмқ„ л‘җм—ҲлӢӨ. к·ёлҠ” 1216л…„ 2мӣ” 15мқј мҳҒкөӯ мһүкёҖлһңл“ң м„ңлЁём„ёнҠё лё”лһҳк·ёлҚҳм—җм„ң 47м„ёмқҳ лӮҳмқҙлЎң мӮ¬л§қн–ҲлӢӨ.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Fitz Martin nasceu aproximadamente 1170, em Dartington, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William Fitz Martin Lord of Cemais, tinha 37 anos e sua mГЈe, Angharad Ankaret ferch Rhys, tinha 32 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Avice de BrГ©autГ©. Ele faleceu em 15 de fevereiro de 1216, em Blagdon, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 47 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: РҡРҫРіРҙР° William Fitz Martin СҖРҫРҙРёР»СҒСҸ РҫРәРҫР»Рҫ 1170 Рі. РІ РјРөСҒСӮРҪРҫСҒСӮРё РҙР°СҖСӮРёРҪРіСӮРҫРҪ, РҙРөРІРҫРҪ, РҗРҪРіР»РёСҸ, Р’РөР»РёРәРҫРұСҖРёСӮР°РҪРёСҸ, РөРіРҫ РҫСӮСҶСғ РҝРҫ РёРјРөРҪРё William Fitz Martin Lord of Cemais РұСӢР»Рҫ 37 Р»РөСӮ, Р° РјР°СӮРөСҖРё, РәРҫСӮРҫСҖСғСҺ Р·РІР°Р»Рё Angharad Ankaret ferch Rhys, 32. РЈ РҪРөРіРҫ РұСӢР» РҝРҫ РәСҖР°Р№РҪРөР№ РјРөСҖРө 1 СҒСӢРҪ РҫСӮ Avice de BrГ©autГ©. РһРҪ СғРјРөСҖ 15 С„РөРІСҖР°Р»СҸ 1216 Рі. РІ РјРөСҒСӮРҪРҫСҒСӮРё РұР»Р°РіРҙРҫРҪ, СҒРҫРјРёСҖСҒРөСӮ, РҗРҪРіР»РёСҸ, Р’РөР»РёРәРҫРұСҖРёСӮР°РҪРёСҸ РІ РІРҫР·СҖР°СҒСӮРө 47 Р»РөСӮ.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Fitz Martin naciГі aproximadamente 1170, en Dartington, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papГЎ, William Fitz Martin Lord of Cemais, tenГӯa 37 aГұos y su mamГЎ, Angharad Ankaret ferch Rhys, tenГӯa 32 aГұos. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Avice de BrГ©autГ©. MuriГі el 15 de febrero de 1216, en Blagdon, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 47 aГұos.
